skill.name: organize-google-contacts
skill.description: Use Rarefriend MCP to search, create, update, tag, and annotate Google Contacts from an AI assistant.
skill.summary: Manage Google Contacts with Rarefriend notes, tags, search, imports, and relationship context.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, MCP tool examples, and documented client configuration paths. The real risk is moderate: this community skill asks users to run a third-party MCP package, send contact data through Rarefriend, and store OAuth client credentials in local MCP configuration.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Contact Data Access
medium_findings.description: The skill connects Google Contacts through Rarefriend and exposes contact search, notes, tags, imports, and sync status workflows. This is a legitimate purpose, but it involves sensitive personal contact data and an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes Rarefriend access to Google Contacts and OAuth connection flows. No evidence of malicious exfiltration was found, but the data sensitivity is clear.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Package Execution
medium_findings.description: Setup instructions tell users to run a Rarefriend MCP server through npx. The package name is fixed and relevant to the skill, but community users must trust code fetched and executed from the npm ecosystem.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in setup documentation and installs a third-party MCP package. It appears intentional and not hidden, so the concern is supply-chain trust rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Local Credential Configuration
medium_findings.description: The setup flow stores Rarefriend OAuth client ID and client secret values in MCP configuration or environment variables. This is common for MCP setup, but it can expose credentials if config files are shared or logged.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential names and config examples are explicit. The risk depends on user handling of local config files, so it is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick executions in SKILL.md and references/SETUP.md. These are Markdown inline code spans, fenced examples, and MCP tool signatures, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown documentation and the flagged backticks delimit text examples. No Ruby source file or backtick execution context exists in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detector False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography on lines that contain front matter, OAuth labels, file paths, or product instructions. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak crypto implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows no cryptographic algorithm usage. The likely trigger is wording around client secrets or configuration, not crypto code.

low_findings.title: Hidden Config Paths Are Documented Client Locations
low_findings.description: Hidden path findings refer to standard MCP configuration files for Cursor and Claude Desktop. The paths are not used by code in this skill, but users should avoid publishing these files because they may contain credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documentation for known application config files. They are not hidden filesystem access by executable skill code, but credential handling remains a minor operational concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx-Based MCP Server Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup path runs a third-party MCP package with npx. This is transparent and relevant, but users should review package provenance before running it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The installation command is explicit and fixed to the Rarefriend MCP package. The risk is external code execution through package installation, not hidden command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: OAuth Contact Sync Through External Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to connect Google Contacts to Rarefriend and then use MCP tools over the synced data. This should be disclosed because contacts are sensitive personal information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The OAuth connection and sync workflow are clearly documented. No hidden endpoint is present, but the privacy impact is direct.

content.value_statement: Contact lists lose value when notes, tags, and history are scattered. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code manage Google Contacts through Rarefriend.
actual_capabilities: Search contacts by name, company, role, email, tag, source, or exact match.
Create, update, delete, and bulk import contact records through Rarefriend MCP tools.
Add, edit, delete, pin, list, and search notes attached to contacts.
List, add, remove, and rename tags across contact records.
Start Google Contacts connection through a Rarefriend OAuth link.
Check Google Contacts sync status before using synced records.
limitations: Requires a Rarefriend account and configured Rarefriend MCP credentials.
Google Contacts access depends on OAuth approval and successful sync.
Bulk contact creation is limited to 50 contacts per call.
The skill does not include calendar, Outlook, LinkedIn, reminders, or WhatsApp workflows.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Sales Call
use_cases.description: Find a contact, review recent notes, and add a follow-up note after the conversation.

use_cases.title: Organize an Investor Network
use_cases.description: Search contacts by company, tag investors, and keep relationship notes for future outreach.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Synced Contacts
use_cases.description: Connect Google Contacts, review synced records, and update names, roles, companies, and tags.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my contact named [name] and show the source, email, company, and role if available.

prompt_templates.title: Add Meeting Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Add this note to [contact name]: [meeting summary]. Pin it if it is important for future follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Tag a Group
prompt_templates.prompt: Find everyone at [company] and tag each matching contact as [tag]. Ask before changing more than 10 contacts.

prompt_templates.title: Import and Review Contacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Import this contact list into Rarefriend, split it into batches of 50, then summarize duplicates and missing fields.

output_examples.input: Who do I know at Stripe?
output_examples.output: Found three matching contacts. Each result includes name, role, company, source, and available email details.

output_examples.input: Tag everyone at Sequoia as investor.
output_examples.output: Matched the Sequoia contacts, applied the investor tag, and reported any records that needed review.

output_examples.input: What did I discuss with Alex last month?
output_examples.output: Returned recent notes for Alex, highlighted the most relevant discussion points, and suggested follow-up context.

best_practices: Review contact details with the user before updating or deleting records.
Mention the source label when presenting contact search results.
Use pinned notes only for context that should stay visible during future work.
anti_patterns: Do not bulk edit contacts without confirming the matched records first.
Do not paste OAuth client secrets into shared chats, tickets, or public files.
Do not assume Google Contacts sync is complete before checking sync status.

faq.question: Does this skill require Rarefriend?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires a Rarefriend account and the Rarefriend MCP server.

faq.question: Can it connect Google Contacts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can start the Google Contacts OAuth connection through Rarefriend and check sync status.

faq.question: Can it search notes across all contacts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents full-text note search across all contacts.

faq.question: Can it import many contacts at once?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports bulk imports, with a documented maximum of 50 contacts per call.

faq.question: Does it manage calendar events?
faq.answer: No. Calendar workflows are described as outside this skill.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing credentials?
faq.answer: Users should keep MCP configuration files private because they may contain Rarefriend client secrets.
